text:
1 INTRODUCTION

<<CLIENT>> recognizes the importance of providing its employees/ end-users with appropriate awareness and training on information security, business continuity and data privacy requirements to protect confidentiality, integrity, availability, privacy and resiliency of its information and communications technology (ICT) systems. This is to protect information assets, resources and systems. The success of <<CLIENT>>’s awareness and training program, and the overall awareness of secure business practices, depends upon the awareness, behavior and culture of the organization.

text:
2 PURPOSE

The purpose of this Security Awareness and Training Policy (“Policy”) is to define a framework for security awareness and training processes to ensure that a proactive and risk-aware information security culture exists within <<CLIENT>>.
Further, this Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, UAE Federal Law 45, ISO 20000, ISO 27001, ISO 22301, and ISO 27701.
The following are the key objectives of this Policy:
    • Security Awareness and Training Program: Develop and implement organization wide awareness and training program through identification of information security training needs across awareness, behavior and culture.
    • Information Security Training: Educate all end-users in <<CLIENT>> to follow information security best practices to ensure protection of confidentiality, integrity, availability, privacy and resiliency of information assets.
    • Measurement and Evaluation: Establish measurements and evaluation methods to determine effectiveness of the security awareness and training program.
    • Awareness Campaign: Establish an awareness campaign in order to ensure a risk-aware information security culture.

3 SCOPE

This Policy applies to all employees, contractors and third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.

text:
4 POLICY STATEMENT

<<CLIENT>> shall develop, implement, and maintain an awareness and training program to inform all employees of the information security, business continuity and data privacy risks associated with their activities.  
Further, <<CLIENT>> shall ensure that employees and third parties are adequately trained to carry out their assigned information security responsibilities.

meta.source_file: /home/puneetha/Documents/IterativeResearch/feb6/regulatory_parsing_improved/data/02_processed/policies/clientname-IS-POL-00-Security Awareness and Training Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping_for_mapping.json

text:
5.1 Awareness and Training Program

<<CLIENT>> shall: 
            5.1.1 Develop and formalize an Information Security (IS) awareness and training program that ensures continued awareness and competence of all <<CLIENT>> employees, including contractors, third-parties, and all other stakeholders with access to information assets. 
            5.1.2 Ensure that Information Security awareness and training program raise awareness and compliance about <<CLIENT>>’s Integrated Management System and its associated policies, procedures, processes, and other best practices. 
            5.1.3 Conduct Information Security awareness and training program to achieve high cybersecurity maturity levels and mitigate risks around lack of awareness.
            5.1.4 Ensure that the content of Information Security awareness and training program is reviewed and updated annually based on the results of the previous awareness activities and campaigns, and lessons learnt from security incidents.
            5.1.5 Ensure that the Information Security awareness and training program provides sufficient knowledge to all <<CLIENT>> employees, and where applicable, to contractors, third-parties to recognize security incidents and to respond to them using the appropriate reporting channels.
            5.1.6 For all new hires, provide induction training that covers information security awareness, to understand its organizational policies and the importance of security with respect to their line of work.
            5.1.7 Provided regular training to its technical staff members to implement, configure and maintain security in the information systems using appropriate safeguards.
            5.1.8 Promote a culture of security through awareness campaigns.
            5.1.9 Utilize security awareness techniques (e.g., displaying digital posters, generating email advisories/ notices from senior organizational officials, phishing simulations etc.) to execute IS campaigns.

5.1 Awareness and Training Program

<<CLIENT>> shall:

1. Develop and formalize an Information Security (IS) awareness and training program that ensures continued awareness and competence of all <<CLIENT>> employees, including contractors, third-parties, and all other stakeholders with access to information assets.
2. Ensure that Information Security awareness and training program raise awareness and compliance about <<CLIENT>>’s Integrated Management System and its associated policies, procedures, processes, and other best practices.
3. Conduct Information Security awareness and training program to achieve high cybersecurity maturity levels and mitigate risks around lack of awareness.
4. Ensure that the content of Information Security awareness and training program is reviewed and updated annually based on the results of the previous awareness activities and campaigns, and lessons learnt from security incidents.
5. Ensure that the Information Securi

meta.source_file: /home/puneetha/Documents/IterativeResearch/feb6/regulatory_parsing_improved/data/02_processed/policies/clientname-IS-POL-00-Security Awareness and Training Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping_for_mapping.json

text:
5.2 Role Specific Training

<<CLIENT>> shall:
            5.2.1 Provide Role specific security training for employees/contractors with assigned administrative or technical roles and responsibilities involving access to sensitive information systems.
            5.2.2 Administer Role specific security training before granting employees/contractors privileged access to the information systems or before employees/contractors begin performing assigned duties. 
5.2.3 Define frequency for Role specific security trainings (e.g. whenever there is a significant change in information system environment or procedures, and whenever an employee/contractor enters a new position that requires additional role-specific training).

5.2 Role Specific Training

<<CLIENT>> shall:

1. Provide Role specific security training for employees/contractors with assigned administrative or technical roles and responsibilities involving access to sensitive information systems.
2. Administer Role specific security training before granting employees/contractors privileged access to the information systems or before employees/contractors begin performing assigned duties.
3. Define frequency for Role specific security trainings (e.g. whenever there is a significant change in information system environment or procedures, and whenever an employee/contractor enters a new position that requires additional role-specific training).

text:
5.3 Training Records

<<CLIENT>> shall:
            5.3.1 Document and monitor individual security training activities, including basic security awareness training and any Role specific security training.
            5.3.2 Retain training records for appropriate periods as defined by [<<CLIENT>>-ORG-IMS-POL-013-Records Retention and Protection Policy].

5.3 Training Records

<<CLIENT>> shall:

1. Document and monitor individual security training activities, including basic security awareness training and any Role specific security training.
2. Retain training records for appropriate periods as defined by [<<CLIENT>>-ORG-IMS-POL-013-Records Retention and Protection Policy].

text:
6 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

Executive & Senior Management
    1 Commit to provide the required resources and support for implementing a successful Information Security awareness and training program. Required resources shall include financial, technical, and human capital needed for all activities planned for the program.
    2 Where relevant, provide direction to and participate in reviews and oversight of Information Security awareness and training program.
    3 Mandate participation of all <<CLIENT>> employees in IS awareness activities, as outlined within the Information Security awareness and training program.

Information Security
    1 Prepare yearly Security Awareness and Training program and carry out activities as per the plan.
    2 Ensure to record results and report progress to Executive & Senior Management.
    3 Responsible for the maintenance and update of Information Security awareness and training program.

Audit
    1 Initiate independent reviews of training and awareness campaigns to determine their impact and recommend changes.

Employees, Line Managers and Third Parties (Contractors, Vendors, etc.)
    1 Participate in all awareness and training activities.
    2 Line managers to mandate all employees attend training sessions.
    3 Shall report all violations and security breaches to <<CLIENT>> Information Security Team through the appropriate channels.
    4 Shall acknowledge their understanding and confirm their commitment to follow and comply with all issued policies and procedures.

6 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

Executive & Senior Management

1. Commit to provide the required resources and support for implementing a successful Information Security awareness and training program. Required resources shall include financial, technical, and human capital needed for all activities planned for the program.
2. Where relevant, provide direction to and participate in reviews and oversight of Information Security awareness and training program.
3. Mandate participation of all <<CLIENT>> employees in IS awareness activities, as outlined within the Information Security awareness and training program.

Information Security

1. Prepare yearly Security Awareness and Training program and carry out activities as per the plan.
2. Ensure to record results and report progress to Executive & Senior Management.
3. Responsible for the maintenance and update of Information Security awareness and training program.

text:
7 POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS

This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <<CLIENT>>'s Human Capital, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.
Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure.’

The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure.’

text:
8 MONITORING AND REVIEW

This Policy is developed and owned by the <<CLIENT>> Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO. 
<<CLIENT>> IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment.
Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <<CLIENT>> IS Team at <<Client_ID>>

8 MONITORING AND REVIEW

This Policy is developed and owned by the <<CLIENT>> Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.

<<CLIENT>> IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment.

Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <<CLIENT>> IS Team at <<Client\_ID>>
